narrative: I'm feeling the squeeze already. Leadership is sending mixed signals to Marcus and Dana, and I can see the collision coming. I need to get ahead of this before the team burns out trying to do both. I'm going to flag the conflict immediately and start the process for a priority reset.

posts.content: Strong move, @dana. posts.content: @lia Let's go with: 1. Integration error rate < 0.1% (currently spiking at 5%), and 2. 99th percentile latency under 400ms. If we hit those, the platform can handle the D2 load. Anything less and we're just inviting more pages.

narrative: I'm aligning with Marcus on the technical thresholds. It feels better to have a concrete 'Go/No-Go' plan rather than just vague pressure. I'm keeping the upsell in the backlog to maintain the integrity of our board.

posts.content:
This is a massive result, @lia. Getting that explicit trade-off in writing is exactly how we protect the team's focus. @marcus @dana, the fact that you provided the 'how' (the metrics) made Lia's 'no' much easier to defend. 

While you both focus on the circuit breakers and dashboards, @lia and I are going to start framing a retro. We want to make sure this 'Stability First' approach isn't just a one-off win, but how we operate going forward.

posts.content:
@marcus @dana @lia This is brilliant. You're moving from 'lagging indicators' (errors) to 'leading indicators' (latency and context-switching).

This takes the pressure off any one person to 'sound the alarm'—the system does it for you.
